title: ugetreg
text: ugetreg Get the integer value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). long ugetreg (shortnum); Copy Parameters num Number of the Uniface general variable. Allowed values are 1 through 99. Return Values The integer value of the specified general variable. If the value of num is out of range, this function returns 1. However, nnote that this is also the return value when num is within the range and the general variable has the value 1. Use Library: yrtl Description ugetreg returns the integer value of the specified Uniface general variable (historically also known as a ‘dollar register’), or 1 (one) if the specified register does not exist. If the general variable does not contain a numeric value, the return value is zero. If the general variable contains a non-integer numeric value, the fractional part is dropped and only the integer part is returned. The following code fragment retrieves the integer value of the contents of the general variable $10 : Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> int value = ugetreg(10); Copy Related Links ugetregf Get the double-precision value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). ugetregs Get the string value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). uputreg Copy a long integer value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99).
